hero.label: Real company partnership · Turkish industrial investment
hero.title: Own shares in production companies.
hero.lead: ORTAQ presents export-oriented manufacturing SMEs through field-reviewed dossiers. What you buy: equity in a real factory — capital works on capacity, machines, and export growth.
hero.conviction: Passive assets wait on price. Productive companies build value through capacity, export, and operational improvement — shareholders participate in that growth. No return guarantee; this is long-term industrial partnership.

opportunities.label: Investment opportunities
opportunities.title: Export-oriented production companies
opportunities.lead: CNC, food, logistics, chemicals, textile — field-reviewed equity partnership dossiers. Each represents a real production facility — not a startup card.
opportunities.capitalLead: Each dossier states where capital goes: line expansion, export infrastructure, machine renewal. Your money works in the factory — operational growth financing, not speculation.

sectors.label: Production economy
sectors.title: Industrial investment · sector diversity
sectors.lead: Each sector carries a different capacity, export, and operational growth story — production-based value creation, unlike passive assets.

process.label: Investment journey
process.title: From discovery to value creation — six steps
process.subtitle: The individual investor path: read dossiers, verify, participate, monitor company performance.

1.title: Discovery
1.text: Browse investment opportunities — company dossiers enriched with production, export, and field notes.

2.title: Review
2.text: Read capacity, risk, machine park, capital use plan, and review milestones in depth.

3.title: Verification
3.text: Understand the SPK framework, licensed platform, escrow account, and MKK share registry.

4.title: Participation
4.text: When ready, acquire shares via the licensed platform — funds stay in escrow, no rush.

5.title: Monitoring
5.text: Track your partnership through field updates, operational traces, and company reports.

6.title: Value creation
6.text: Capacity growth, export expansion, margin improvement — company value develops with operational performance. Dividends, appreciation, or exit vary by campaign; no guarantee.

ownership.label: Partnership model
ownership.title: In 60 seconds: what do you own?
ownership.lead: Not speculation — equity in a real company. Capital works in the production economy.

1.title: Share ownership
1.text: When the campaign completes, shares registered in your name at MKK — partnership in an industrial company not listed on the public exchange.

2.title: Role of capital
2.text: Your money funds capacity expansion, machine investment, export infrastructure, or operational bottlenecks — stated clearly in each dossier.

3.title: How value forms
3.text: Production volume, export share, margins, and company value can grow through operational improvement. The investor owns a share of that table.

4.title: Return expectations
4.text: Dividends, appreciation, or strategic exit — specified in the campaign information form. No return guarantee; you can lose money.

ownership.examples: Capacity expansion · export growth · machine renewal · new production line · regional expansion · margin improvement

wealth.title: Why production partnership?

most.3: Public markets — liquid but broad index

few.3: Value accumulation through capacity and machines
few.4: A layer historically closed to individual access

wealth.barrier: Due diligence cost, closed networks, and fragmented process kept this layer away from individual investors. ORTAQ makes access visible through disciplined dossiers and verification.
wealth.positioning: A serious bridge between individual investors and Turkish industrial growth — operational partnership, not hype.

activity.label: Operational momentum
activity.title: Companies growing from the field

why.label: Why it matters
why.title: Most people cannot access the productive economy.
why.lead: In Turkey, individual capital mostly flows to financial assets. Yet employment, export, and capacity are built in factories.
why.fewLabel: What almost no one can access
why.barrier: This layer stayed opaque: due diligence is costly, access is closed, process is fragmented. Outside large funds and local networks, individual participation is nearly impossible.
why.ortaq: ORTAQ makes this layer visible through dossiers, field inspection, and verification discipline — without rushing, transparently.

2.text: Field visit — operational reality on site

1.text: Read the dossier. Understand the company, production, risks.

2.text: When ready, partnership begins via the licensed platform.

3.text: Funds stay in escrow. Shares open in MKK.

homeReality.label: Real economy
homeReality.title: Not paper — production lines.
homeReality.lead: Turkey's economic weight sits in factories, workshops, export warehouses, and machine parks — not only on the exchange. ORTAQ makes that layer visible.

production.label: Production
production.title: Machine noise, real value.
production.text: CNC lines, welding bays, quality control — equity partnership is built on these systems. Not abstract finance — physical production.

export.label: Export
export.title: Cross-border movement.
export.text: Containers, loading ramps, dispatch paperwork. Parts made in Anatolia reach Europe and Central Asia. Partnership sits inside that movement.

people.label: People
people.title: Operator, founder, engineer.
people.text: Real companies are run by real people. ORTAQ presents founder story, field visit, and document pack together.

homeWhy.label: Why it matters
homeWhy.title: Gold is easy. FX is easy. Productive shares are not.
homeWhy.p1: In Turkey, savings mostly flow to gold, FX, and property. Owning a stake in exporting, producing companies stayed closed, opaque, and hard to access.
homeWhy.p2: ORTAQ aims to make equity partnership understandable, visible, and disciplined. Not a revolution — infrastructure. Trust before speed. Selectivity before scale.
homeWhy.p3: Not every company enters the file. Not every application becomes a campaign.
homeWhy.quote: Partnership starts on the factory floor — not on paper.

homeSelection.label: Selection
homeSelection.title: Not every company enters.
homeSelection.lead: ORTAQ is not a showcase — it is a review gate. Preliminary application, document pack, field visit, financial and legal review, committee approval. No rush.

1.title: Preliminary application
1.text: Sector fit, basic financial profile, and partnership purpose are assessed.

2.title: Document pack
2.text: Trade registry, tax records, management reports, and export documents are collected.

3.title: Field visit
3.text: Production line, machine park, and operational reality are verified on site.

4.title: Committee decision
4.text: After financial, legal, and operational review, a publish decision is made.

homeTrust.label: Trust architecture
homeTrust.title: Seriousness comes from clarity.
homeTrust.lead: Trust is not a slogan — it is an operational layer. Every step is documented, every claim is labelled, every process is transparent.

1.title: Review layer
1.text: No company is published without document pack and financial disclosure review. Status is labelled at every step.

2.title: Licensed platform
2.text: Retail partnership only runs via an SPK-listed crowdfunding platform. Partner selection is ongoing.

3.title: Escrow account
3.text: Until the campaign ends, funds stay in escrow. They do not go directly to the company.

4.title: MKK registry
4.text: After the capital increase, your shares appear in MKK records under your name.

homeAccess.label: Layer 06
homeAccess.title: Maybe there is a place for you here.
homeAccess.text: No rush yet. But the door is opening — slow, serious, real.
homeAccess.note: No transactions yet. Discovery and preparation phase.

about.label: ORTAQ
about.title: What ORTAQ is and is not
about.is: ORTAQ is an interface that explains equity crowdfunding in plain language. For people who want partnership in real companies.
about.isNot: ORTAQ is not a bank, not an adviser, and does not promise returns. Decision and risk are yours.

money.label: Money
money.title: Where does your money go?
money.intro: The most common question. In short:

1.title: When you join
1.text: Funds go to an escrow account. Not to the company.

2.title: During the campaign
2.text: Funds stay blocked. The company cannot use them.

3.title: When the campaign ends
3.text: If the target is met, capital increase proceeds. If not, refund process applies.

4.title: After that
4.text: Shares are registered at MKK in your name.

money.note: ORTAQ does not hold funds. Escrow is set up within the licensed platform framework.

risk.label: Risks
risk.title: Know the risks
risk.intro: You can lose money in partnership. We do not hide that.
risk.ack: I have read the risk items. I know I may lose everything, cannot withdraw quickly, and annual limits apply.

process.label: Process
process.title: What comes next?
process.intro: If a platform agreement is in place, the flow is roughly:

wait.label: For now
wait.title: No transactions yet
wait.text: ORTAQ does not process transactions yet. You are here to read. That is normal.
wait.emptyText: When the licensed platform agreement is final, you will continue from here. Reading is enough for now.
wait.partnerNote: Platform agreement not yet in place
wait.delay: If there is a delay, we will say so on this page. Nothing opens silently.

risk.label: Know this first
risk.title: Every partnership carries risk
risk.intro: ORTAQ aims to align the process with regulation; it does not remove risk.

items.1: You may lose your entire contribution.
items.2: Money does not return quickly; exit can take years.
items.3: The company may miss or delay targets.
items.4: Annual participation limits apply. Amounts are in the information memorandum and regulation.
items.5: ORTAQ does not give investment advice. The decision is yours.

risk.cooling: Legal cooling-off applies during the campaign. Terms are in the information memorandum.
risk.preface: A short list to read before deciding.

process.label: Participation process
process.title: How equity investment is completed
process.intro: Discovery and review begin in ORTAQ dossiers; company selection passes six-stage due diligence; participation completes on the licensed platform under SPK regulation. Five steps summarise the legal partnership flow.
process.journeyNote: ORTAQ provides discovery, review, and verification; company selection passes six-stage due diligence; transactions run on the licensed platform.

1.title: Sign up
1.text: e-Government identity verification and risk disclosure — your investor identity is formalised.

2.title: Review the company
2.text: Information memorandum, capital use plan, and where your money goes.

3.title: Join the campaign
3.text: Funds go to escrow — not transferred to the company until the campaign closes.

4.title: Capital increase
4.text: If target is met, the transaction completes; company capital strengthens and you become a shareholder.

5.title: Share registry
5.text: Shares open at MKK in your name. You then monitor operational updates — value creation depends on company performance.

cta.title: Understand first, decide later.
cta.text: Partnership is not rushed. Reading takes minutes; your decision may be long-term.

footer.tagline: Disciplined discovery layer for Turkish industrial investment — a clear path to partnership in real companies.
footer.regulatory: Equity crowdfunding requires annual limits, information memorandum, risk disclosure, and cooling-off rights for individual participants. Transactions run on an SPK-regulated licensed platform.
footer.notAdvice: ORTAQ does not provide investment advice. Content is for information only; returns are not guaranteed.

faqTeaser.label: Investment questions
faqTeaser.title: Clear answers on partnership, returns, and risk

footer.dispute: Complaints are accepted in writing and reviewed alongside licensed platform procedures.
footer.entityNote: Company details and trade registry information will be published here when complete.

journey.label: Investor journey
journey.note: Discovery → evaluation → trust → partnership. ORTAQ guides you step by step — without rush, through dossier reading.

evaluationPage.label: Company selection
evaluationPage.title: How are companies evaluated?
evaluationPage.lead: Not every application is accepted. ORTAQ files only manufacturing companies with verifiable operations and long-term economic logic — six-stage due diligence before investors see a dossier.
evaluationPage.leadSecondary: This is the verification leg of the investor journey: after discovery, before participation. Trust layers and the participation process build on this review.
evaluationPage.journeyContext: After evaluation, read the trust framework, then proceed to the participation process.

1.title: Initial screening
1.text: Sector fit, operating history, production or export proof, basic financials.
1.reject: One-person consultancies, speculative projects, undocumented claims.

2.title: Operational review
2.text: Factory or production site visit, machine park, order book, supply chain.

3.title: Legal and registry
3.text: Trade registry, MERSİS, tax compliance, ownership structure, litigation search.

4.title: Financial review
4.text: Revenue trend, debt structure, cash flow, capital use plan consistency.

5.title: Founder interview
5.text: In-person or video interview. Operational knowledge, goals, risk awareness.
5.reject: Misleading statements, press pressure, unrealistic growth claims.

6.title: Committee and platform
6.text: Finance, field, and legal vote. Licensed crowdfunding platform final approval.

trustPage.title: Trust and investor protection
trustPage.subtitle: Serious investment layer: we do not publish unproven claims. From discovery to evaluation, from trust framework to participation — legal protection and operational transparency at every step.
trustPage.journeyNote: After reading company selection, verify trust layers here; then proceed to the participation process.

riskPage.title: Risks and limits
riskPage.subtitle: Read before deciding on partnership.
riskPage.notAdvice: This page is not investment advice.

1.title: Principal risk
1.text: You may lose everything. No return guarantee.

2.title: Liquidity
2.text: You may not sell when you want. Exit can take years.

3.title: Retail limits
3.text: Non-qualified individuals face annual limits per regulation.

4.title: Cooling-off
4.text: Legal cooling-off during the campaign per information memorandum.

5.title: Tax
5.text: Partnership may create tax obligations. Consult your accountant.

6.title: Company risk
6.text: The company may fail, miss targets, or change management.

7.title: Platform role
7.text: ORTAQ provides an interface; it does not guarantee company performance.

help.title: Questions
help.text: If unsure, write us or consult an independent adviser before deciding.

privacy.p1: ORTAQ processes personal data under Turkish KVKK.
privacy.p2: e-Government verification uses data only for verification.
privacy.p3: Full text published after legal review before platform partnership goes live.
